commit_scope: Move GET /api/health from native-compatible shape toward native operational truth

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/json.fk form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk form-stdlib/tests/kernel-http-band.fk
ssh tunnel opened per docs/PRODUCTION-SUBSTRATE.md to local 127.0.0.1:15432 using the local kernel config overlay
curl -sS -H 'Accept: application/json' 'http://127.0.0.1:19086/api/health' | jq '{status, schema_ok, smart_reap_available, kernel_runtime, recent_outcomes}'
temporary Go listener and SSH tunnel stopped; lsof confirmed no listeners on 19086 or 15432
local_validation.summary: The route-goal read showed current web_api traffic at 47/47 events on GET /api/health, already high-grammar native, so this movement served health truth rather than route-count coverage. The BML health handler now populates recent_outcomes from production runtime_events when the native kernel has DB reach, keeps null when that organ cannot answer, and resolves smart_reap_available from repo_root instead of process cwd. The Go route test proves the native listener still compiles and serves the operational shape.

ci_validation.reason: No commit, push, or PR was created in this turn.

deploy_validation.reason: A local Go kernel listener hit production Postgres through the documented SSH tunnel and returned native 200 for GET /api/health with populated recent_outcomes.

phase_gate.reason: Local and production-tunnel proof passed; CI and public deployment remain pending until this work is committed, pushed, and shipped.

evidence_refs: deploy/front-door/api.bml defines api-recent-outcomes-* helpers that aggregate recent runtime_events into the existing health response shape.
deploy/front-door/api.bml now resolves smart_reap_available from repo_root, avoiding cwd-sensitive false negatives in the Go kernel.
form/form-kernel-go/server_test.go includes TestHealthRouteNativeOperationalShape for the native listener route shape.
Native production-tunnel curl returned X-Form-Router: native-kernel-go with schema_ok=true, smart_reap_available=true, kernel_runtime=form-kernel-go, and populated recent_outcomes.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: GET /api/health served by the native Go kernel now reports recent_outcomes from the persisted runtime_events organ when production DB reach is present, while preserving the existing health response contract and keeping unavailable observations null.
test_flows: Go test suite compiles the BML front-door catalog and asserts native /api/health response fields and route headers.
Native Go listener served /api/health against production Postgres through the documented SSH tunnel.
Public API and native health samples both reported status=ok, schema_ok=true, smart_reap_available=true, and populated recent_outcomes; kernel_runtime differs by carrier as expected.
known_gaps: The native route reads deployed_sha from config, so the local production-tunnel overlay reports null until that overlay carries deployed_sha.
A broader health domain grammar can emerge later if shape_health, provider readiness, witness organs, and runtime outcome summaries repeat enough structure.
